title: Vera Cruz, Bahia

infobox.value: is a [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Brazil "Municipalities of Brazil") in the [state](/wiki/States_of_Brazil "States of Brazil") of [Bahia](/wiki/Bahia "Bahia") in the [North-East region](/wiki/Nordeste "Nordeste") of [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). It occupies 87% of the island of [Itaparica](/wiki/Itaparica_Island "Itaparica Island"); the remainder belongs to the municipality of [Itaparica](/wiki/Itaparica,_Bahia "Itaparica, Bahia"). The municipality of Vera Cruz has a population of is 43,716 (2020 estimate) and covers an area of 297.537 square kilometres (114.880 mi2).

["Ranking decrescente do IDH-M dos municípios do Brasil"](https://web.archive.org/web/20091003103229/http://www.pnud.org.br/atlas/ranking/IDH-M%2091%2000%20Ranking%20decrescente%20%28pelos%20dados%20de%202000%29.htm) (in Portuguese). Atlas do Desenvolvimento Humano, Programa das Nações Unidas para o Desenvolvimento (PNUD). 2000. Archived from [the original](http://www.pnud.org.br/atlas/ranking/IDH-M%2091%2000%20Ranking%20decrescente%20(pelos%20dados%20de%202000).htm) on October 3, 2009. Retrieved December 17, 2009.
["Vera Cruz"](https://hpip.org/en/Contents/Place/91). Lisbon, Portugal: Heritage of Portuguese Influence/Património de Influência Portuguesa. 2012. Retrieved 2021-03-03.

**Vera Cruz (Bahia)** is a [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Brazil "Municipalities of Brazil") in the [state](/wiki/States_of_Brazil "States of Brazil") of [Bahia](/wiki/Bahia "Bahia") in the [North-East region](/wiki/Nordeste "Nordeste") of [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"). It occupies 87% of the island of [Itaparica](/wiki/Itaparica_Island "Itaparica Island"); the remainder belongs to the municipality of [Itaparica](/wiki/Itaparica,_Bahia "Itaparica, Bahia"). The municipality of Vera Cruz has a population of is 43,716 (2020 estimate) and covers an area of 297.537 square kilometres (114.880 mi2).

The municipality is located 5.5 kilometres (3.4 mi) opposite of Salvador across the [Bay of All Saints](/wiki/Bay_of_All_Saints "Bay of All Saints").[[2]](#cite_note-i-2)[[3]](#cite_note-PNUD_IDH_2000-3)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vera_Cruz,_Bahia&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The island of Itaparica was home to a large population of [Tupinambá peoples](/wiki/Tupinamb%C3%A1_people "Tupinambá people") prior to the arrival of the Portugues. [Diogo Álvaro Corrêa](/wiki/Caramuru "Caramuru") (c. 1475-1557), a Portuguese navigator also known as Caramuru, arrived on Itaparica in 1510. He met [Catarina Paraguaçu](/wiki/Catarina_Paragua%C3%A7u "Catarina Paraguaçu") (died 1586), daughter of the Tupinambá chief Taparica, and married her. Catarina travelled with Caramuru to France in 1526, was baptized in 1528, and is noted for her [Marian](/wiki/Mary_(mother_of_Jesus) "Mary (mother of Jesus)") visions.[[2]](#cite_note-i-2)

Portuguese settlements in Vera Cruz were established soon after the arrival of [Tomé de Sousa](/wiki/Tom%C3%A9_de_Sousa "Tomé de Sousa"), Brazil's first governor-general, in 1549. The [Jesuits](/wiki/Jesuits "Jesuits") founded a small settlement in present-day Baiacu in 1560. It was called the Vila do Senhor da Vera Cruz, and featured Brazil's first hydraulic works, a dam to provide drinking water. The ruins of the Jesuit church remain on the site. Sugarcane, wheat, and cattle were introduced to the area in the same period. The wealth of the island led to attacks by English corsairs by 1597. It was invaded by the Dutch between the years 1600 and 1647.[[2]](#cite_note-i-2)

The Island of Itaparica was administered as part of Salvador until 1833. It was elevated to city status in 1962, and subsequently divided into two municipalities: Vera Cruz and Itaparica.[[2]](#cite_note-i-2)

## Historic structures

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vera_Cruz,_Bahia&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Historic structures")]

Vera Cruz his home to numerous colonial-era structures, many dating to the 16th century. All lack federal, state, or municipal protection, with the exception of the Chapel of Saint Antony of Velásquez.

* [Chapel of Our Lady of Penha](/w/index.php?title=Chapel_of_Our_Lady_of_Penha&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chapel of Our Lady of Penha (page does not exist)")
* [Chapel of Saint Antony of Velásquez](/w/index.php?title=Chapel_of_Saint_Antony_of_Vel%C3%A1squez&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chapel of Saint Antony of Velásquez (page does not exist)")
* [Parish Church of Santo Amaro](/w/index.php?title=Parish_Church_of_Santo_Amaro&action=edit&redlink=1 "Parish Church of Santo Amaro (page does not exist)")
* [Church of Nosso Senhor da Vera Cruz](/w/index.php?title=Church_of_Nosso_Senhor_da_Vera_Cruz&action=edit&redlink=1 "Church of Nosso Senhor da Vera Cruz (page does not exist)")
* [Church of Our Lady of the Conception](/w/index.php?title=Church_of_Our_Lady_of_the_Conception_(Vera_Cruz)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Church of Our Lady of the Conception (Vera Cruz) (page does not exist)")
* [Mercês Windmill](/w/index.php?title=Merc%C3%AAs_Windmill&action=edit&redlink=1 "Mercês Windmill (page does not exist)")[[4]](#cite_note-hpip-v-4)
